snippets.title: A typed CLI with a flag
snippets.code:
import argparse

def main() -> None:
    p = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Embed a folder of docs")
    p.add_argument("path", help="folder to ingest")
    p.add_argument("--model", default="text-embedding-3-small")
    p.add_argument("--batch-size", type=int, default=64)
    p.add_argument("--dry-run", action="store_true")
    args = p.parse_args()

    print(f"ingest {args.path} model={args.model} batch={args.batch_size} dry={args.dry_run}")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
snippets.explanation: Positional path, typed --batch-size, defaulted --model and a boolean --dry-run, all parsed in one call.

snippets.title: Restricting values with choices
snippets.code:
import argparse

p = argparse.ArgumentParser()
p.add_argument("--model", choices=["gpt-4o", "gpt-4o-mini"], default="gpt-4o")
p.add_argument("--temperature", type=float, default=0.7)
args = p.parse_args(["--model", "gpt-4o-mini"])
print(args.model, args.temperature)   # gpt-4o-mini 0.7
snippets.explanation: choices validates allowed values; passing a list to parse_args is handy for tests.

snippets.title: Subcommands with subparsers
snippets.code:
import argparse

p = argparse.ArgumentParser(prog="rag")
sub = p.add_subparsers(dest="command", required=True)

ingest = sub.add_parser("ingest")
ingest.add_argument("path")

chat = sub.add_parser("chat")
chat.add_argument("--model", default="gpt-4o")

args = p.parse_args(["ingest", "./docs"])
print(args.command, args.path)   # ingest ./docs
snippets.explanation: Each subcommand gets its own arguments; args.command tells you which was chosen.
